title: Old Wounds

{{Quote|There is no Palpatine. No Empire. No Jedi. There is no light. No dark… just you and I here now!|Darth Maul, to Obi-Wan Kenobi|<ref name="Old Wounds" />}}
"'''Old Wounds'''" is a thirteen-page [[Star Wars Legends|non-canon Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written and drawn by [[Aaron McBride]]. The comic was created for the [[2005]] anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]'', which included stories from concept artists who had worked on the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'' Episode III: ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. The comic follows the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], who has gained [[Cyborg/Legends|metallic]] [[Limb/Legends|legs]] following his apparent [[Death/Legends|death]] at the end of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] and seeks revenge on the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. In [[16 BBY/Legends|16 BBY]], Maul tracks him down to the [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars homestead]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where Kenobi reveals himself after Maul threatens [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen]] and [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Beru Lars]], and their [[Skywalker family/Legends|nephew]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Kenobi [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|engages]] Maul in a [[Duel at the Lars homestead|duel]] but hesitates to make the killing move. Owen shoots Maul instead, and Kenobi takes his body away, with Owen instructing him not to return to their [[Moisture farm/Legends|farm]].

"Old Wounds" references a variety of events and characters from the prequel trilogy, and Maul's survival would later be referenced in several works by author [[Abel G. Peña]]. McBride's cyborg design of Maul would later form the basis of the character's appearance in the animated television series ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'', which debuted in the [[2011]] [[The Clone Wars: Season Four|season four]] episode "[[Revenge (episode)|Revenge]]." In [[canon]], [[Maul]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi|Kenobi]] would also have a [[Mission to Tatooine (Imperial Era)|duel]] on [[Tatooine]] during the [[Imperial Era]], ending in Maul's [[death]].

==Publisher's summary==
''"''A dark menace has appeared on Tatooine, and one of the galaxy's last Jedi must stop it. Obi-Wan Kenobi defends the Lars Homestead from an old enemy he did not expect to ever see again.''"<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" />''

==Plot summary==
===Return===
{{Quote|A heart just quickened. I know you're here.|Darth Maul|<ref name="Old Wounds" />}}
During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="Old Wounds" /> in [[16 BBY/Legends|16 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> on the [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars homestead]]<ref name="Old Wounds" /> on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], the [[Moisture farmer/Legends|moisture farmer]] [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen Lars]] and his [[Youngling/Legends|youngling]] [[Skywalker family/Legends|nephew]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] look out at the setting [[Tatoo I/Legends|twin]] [[Tatoo II/Legends|suns]]. Lars encourages Skywalker to practice saying the word [[Moisture vaporator/Legends|vaporator]] and then tells his [[Gender/Legends|nephew]] that they need to be quiet so that the [[Binary star system/Legends|twin suns]] can rest before the next [[Standard day/Legends|day]]. He notices that Skywalker is ready for rest as well, but then sees a figure running toward them in the distance. Lars pulls out his [[Model TD2.3 electrobinoculars/Legends|electrobinoculars]] and sees a [[Sith apparel|robed]] hooded figure with [[Cyborg/Legends|metallic]] [[Limb/Legends|legs]] sprinting across the sands. The farmer calls for<ref name="Old Wounds" /> his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]]<ref name="NEGTC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Beru Lars]] to bring him his [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]]. She asks him if a [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] is approaching, which Owen doubts before instructing Beru to take Luke inside.<ref name="Old Wounds" />

[[File:Maul_blaster_attack.png|thumb|right|250px|Maul attacks Owen Lars.]]
Owen attempts to peacefully ask the figure about their business before ordering them to stop and raising the [[Rifle/Legends|rifle]]. Once the figure—whose [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Tattoo/Legends|tattooed]] arms have become visible—nears Owen, the farmer fires, but the shot hits the ground as the figure leaps upward, [[Force power/Legends|uses]] [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetically]] pull the rifle into his hands, and knocks Owen down with it as he lands. Beru, still outside and holding Skywalker, calls out for her husband and begins to beg for sympathy from the figure. The robed figure, the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], remarks with surprise that there was a son there, before [[Force sense/Legends|sensing]] a [[Heart/Legends|heart]] quickening and realizing that his target, the fugitive [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], is present.<ref name="Old Wounds" />

===Duel===
{{Quote|We can do this for old times' sake. But I was a Padawan then… now… you won't heal clean!|Obi-Wan Kenobi, to Darth Maul|<ref name="Old Wounds" />}}
Maul recounts his journey in tracking his quarry, having missed Kenobi on<ref name="Old Wounds" /> the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] planet<ref name="TEA" /> [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] by two days,<ref name="Old Wounds" /> the Outer Rim planet<ref name="TEA" /> [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] by one day, and encountered the burnt body of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] on<ref name="Old Wounds" /> the Outer Rim planet [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]], which he was relieved to discover was not Kenobi. He then describes how he dissatisfyingly [[Death/Legends|murdered]] [[Kallidahin/Legends|Kallidahin]] on<ref name="Old Wounds" /> the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]]<ref name="TEA" /> [[Polis Massa/Legends|Polis Massa]] for their medical logs and how he killed a greedy elderly [[Junk dealer/Legends|junk dealer]],<ref name="Old Wounds" /> [[Watto/Legends|Watto]],<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|holocron|22/comments|Movie Characters killed in the EU}}</ref> after receiving a name that directed him to the Lars homestead to find Skywalker. Maul correctly believed that by finding the boy, Kenobi would reveal himself. Kenobi asks [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathically]] if<ref name="Old Wounds" /> Maul's [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]]<ref name="NEGTC" /> [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] is aware of Maul's arrval. The [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] replies that there is no Palpatine, Empire, [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], or even [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light]] or [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] that matters, only the conflict between him and Kenobi. The Jedi bursts out from under the ground, declaring that he and Maul can have a [[Duel at the Lars homestead|rematch]] for "old times' sake," but that he was a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] when they [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|fought previously]], and that Maul would not "heal clean."<ref name="Old Wounds" />

[[File:OvMVisionaries.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Obi-Wan and Maul rematch.]]
Maul discards his robes to reveal his full cybernetic appearance and activates his [[Darth Maul's double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]]. Kenobi activates his [[Obi-Wan Kenobi's third lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and Maul rushes toward the Jedi to grab his neck with his metallic leg. The Zabrak pulls Kenobi close and tells him that after falling so far back in [[Theed/Legends|Theed]],<ref name="Old Wounds" /> the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]],<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}}</ref> he became fixated on revenge on Kenobi and has been working toward his revenge every day since. Kenobi breaks out of Maul's grip and the two [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|clash]] blades. Kenobi punches Maul in the head, chipping off several of his horns, and then changes to a reverse grip before leaping to an attack. Meanwhile, Beru crawls toward Owen—who is still recovering from Maul's attack—and calls to him. Kenobi's attack severs Maul's left arm and bisects his [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], causing the Sith to cry out in pain and kick Kenobi backward onto a [[X-34 landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]].<ref name="Old Wounds" />

Maul telekinetically recalls one half of his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and turns toward Kenobi, who has his deactivated [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]] directly in front of Maul's head, ready to deliver the killing blow. The Jedi hesitates to strike, having a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of the deaths of several people close to him, including that of<ref name="Old Wounds" /> his master [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] at Maul's hands,<ref name="TPM" /> fellow Jedi [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]],<ref name="Old Wounds" /> Luke's [[Parent/Legends|mother]]<ref name="ROTS">{{Film|III}}</ref> [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], as well as his former Padawan Anakin Skywalker<ref name="Old Wounds" /> screaming hatred toward him on Mustafar.<ref name="ROTS" /> Kenobi and Maul glare at each other, when suddenly Owen fires his blaster at Maul's head, killing him. Looking at the corpse, Kenobi tells Owen that he will take it out to the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]] to burn it. The Jedi asks if Luke is okay after the ordeal, and Owen reiterates a promise he had previously made to keep the boy safe, even if it meant from Kenobi. Lars then tells Kenobi that he is not to return to the homestead. As Kenobi leaves, carrying Maul's corpse, he telepathically communicates with Luke, telling him that he will always be present even if Luke cannot see him.<ref name="Old Wounds" />

==Development==
{{Quote|Some Sith are harder to kill than others, it would seem.|A caption from the announcement post of ''Star Wars: Visionaries'' on StarWars.com|<ref name="Visionaries announcement">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20040803.html|text=Coming Soon: ''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}</ref>}}
[[File:Old_Wounds_illustrations.jpg|thumb|right|300px|A preliminary illustration of "Old Wounds" against the finished version]]
The thirteen-page comic "Old Wounds" was created for the graphic novel anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''.<ref name="Visionaries" /> The comic was written, inked, pencilled, lettered, and colored<ref name="Tweet" /> by [[Aaron McBride]], one of the concept artists who worked on the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']],<ref name="Visionaries" /> which was released on [[May 19]], [[2005]].<ref name="Year by Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> The concept for ''Visionaries'' was suggested by author [[Jonathan W. Rinzler|J.W. Rinzler]], who wanted a way to honor the numerous ideas from the artists behind the film that would otherwise be unseen by the public.<ref name="Insider 82">{{InsiderCite|82|Drawn by the Force}}</ref> McBride also credited Rinzler as an editor for "Old Wounds."<ref name="Tweet" /> Each artist had freedom to choose whichever aspects of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe they wished to explore.<ref name="Comics Companion">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]''</ref>

''Visionaries'' was announced on [[StarWars.com]] on [[August 3]], [[2004]], in a post that confirmed McBride's involvement and was accompanied by concept art of a cybernetically enhanced Maul,<ref name="Visionaries announcement" /> which would later be included in the graphic novel separate to the stories.<ref name="Visionaries" /> McBride completed his comic in autumn 2004.<ref name="Tweet" /> Another StarWars.com post on [[March 15]], 2005 revealed the titles of the eleven stories in ''Visionaries'' and included a preview image. The caption revealed that Kenobi would fight an old enemy to save the Lars family, but avoided naming Maul specifically.<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" /> The title of "Old Wounds" was intended by McBride to have a double meaning, referring both to Maul's previous injuries and the rift between Kenobi and Owen Lars.<ref name="Insider 82" />

==Release and reception==
[[File:Aaron_McBride.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Aaron McBride wrote and illustrated "Old Wounds."]]
''Visionaries'' was edited by [[Jeremy Barlow]] and published as a trade paperback by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[April 2]], 2005.<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" /> "Old Wounds" serves as the first story in the collection, followed by "[[The Artist of Naboo]]".<ref name="Visionaries" /> Barlow praised the variety and quality of the stories in ''Visionaries'', and felt that the artists involved had justified their positions as designers for<ref name="Insider 82" /> ''Star Wars'' creator<ref name="Year by Year" /> [[George Lucas]].<ref name="Insider 82" /> Rinzler was highly impressed by McBride's art for ''Revenge of the Sith'' and "Old Wounds," which led him to recommend McBride to the staff of ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine to design several of the "Sith edition" covers for the magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 88|eighty-eighth issue]], published in [[June]] [[2006]].<ref name="Insider 88">{{InsiderCite|88|Morbid Visions}}</ref> Author [[Stephen J. Sansweet]] described the comic's concept of Maul's revival as being so well-received that it led to its ideas being adapted for the animated television series ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]''.<ref name="Action Figure">''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Action Figure Collection]]''</ref>

In [[2010]], a re-print of "Old Wounds" was sold within a comic and action figure pack that included figures of Owen Lars and Darth Maul's appearances from the comic. The pack was sold as part of [[Hasbro]]'s [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Dark Side|''Shadows of the Dark Side'']] toy line as a [[Wikipedia:San Diego Comic-Con|San Diego Comic-Con]] exclusive. The comic pack also included "[[Marked]],"<ref name="Hasbro Shadows" /> a comic about Maul<ref name="Marked">{{Tales|24|Marked}}</ref> that was first published in the [[Star Wars Tales 24|twenty-fourth issue]] of the comic anthology series ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' on [[July 13]], 2005.<ref name="Marked date">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/10-968/Star-Wars-Tales-24-photo-cover|text=Star Wars Tales 24}}</ref> The entirety of ''Visionaries'', including "Old Wounds," was later republished by Dark Horse in [[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2|''Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space'' Volume 2]]<ref name="Omnibus" /> on [[October 9]], [[2013]].<ref name="Dark Horse">{{DarkHorse|url=Books/18-450/Star-Wars-Omnibus-Wild-Space-Volume-2-TPB|text=Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2 TPB}}</ref> "Old Wounds" itself was later reprinted by [[Marvel Comics]] in ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Empire Vol. 4]]'',<ref name="Empire V4" /> published on [[May 23]], [[2018]],<ref name="Marvel Empire">{{Marvel|url=comics/collection/68427/star_wars_legends_epic_collection_the_empire_vol_4_trade_paperback|text=Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Empire Vol. 4 (Trade Paperback)}}</ref> and ''[[Star Wars Legends: The Empire Omnibus Vol. 2]]'',<ref name="Empire V2" /> published on [[October 31]], [[2023]].<ref name="Random House">{{PenguinComics|url=book/?isbn=9781302951719|text=Star Wars Legends: THE EMPIRE OMNIBUS VOL. 2 HC}}</ref> In both of the Legends collections, "Old Wounds" was placed between the 2013 miniseries [[Star Wars: Darth Vader and the Cry of Shadows|''Darth Vader and the Cry of Shadows'']] and "[[The Duty]]," a comic story previously published in [[Star Wars Tales 12|''Star Wars Tales'' 12]] in [[2002]].<ref name="Empire V4" /><ref name="Empire V2" />

==Continuity==
===Status and connections to earlier media===
{{Quote|The Darth Maul story is non-continuity,|Leland Chee, on the canon status of "Old Wounds"|<ref name="SWBoards">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=125719&start=0675#10095628|thread=Event Timeline and Character Age Discussion|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2005-03-29|time=11:02 AM}}</ref>}}
[[File:Maul_twix.png|thumb|right|300px|"Old Wounds" presents a non-canon narrative where Maul survived his bisection (pictured) in ''The Phantom Menace''.]]
[[Holocron continuity database]] keeper [[Leland Chee]] confirmed on the [[StarWars.com Message Boards]] on [[March 29]], 2005, that "Old Wounds" would be considered non-canon<ref name="SWBoards" /> within what would later be rebranded as the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity in [[2014]].<ref name="Legends">{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}}</ref> Author [[Pablo Hidalgo]] also stated in the [[Star Wars Q&A|Ask the Master]] column of the [[Star Wars Insider 85|eighty-fifth issue]] of the ''Star Wars Insider'', published in [[November]] 2005, that "Old Wounds" fell under the [[Infinities]] label in response to a fan question.<ref name="Insider 85">{{InsiderCite|85|Star Wars Q&A|Ask the Master}}</ref>

"Old Wounds" follows on from the ending of the [[1999]] prequel trilogy film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], in which Kenobi severed Maul's legs and the Sith apprentice fell<ref name="TPM" /> to his death<ref name="TPM novel">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization]]</ref> into an energy shaft on Naboo.<ref name="TPM" /> Maul's survival had previously been depicted in the ambiguously [[canon]] comic "[[Resurrection (comic story)|Resurrection]]," written by [[Ron Marz]] and published in the [[Star Wars Tales 9|ninth issue]] of ''Star Wars Tales'' in [[2001]]. However, that story [[Duel on Kalakar Six|pitted]] Maul against Darth Vader instead of Kenobi, and the Zabrak was said to have been resurrected by the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]] rather than cybernetically reconstructed.<ref name="Resurrection">{{Tales|9|Resurrection (comic story)}}</ref>

The opening of the comic places it three years into the Imperial occupation, which began in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', placing the events of "Old Wounds" in 16 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], during the [[Rise of the Empire era]].<ref name="Date" /> "Old Wounds" also continues from the ending of ''Revenge of the Sith'', in which Kenobi leaves Luke Skywalker with the Lars family and plans to watch over him.<ref name="ROTS" /> It contains numerous references to the events of the prequel trilogy. In Maul's journey to find Kenobi, he is depicted firstly on a landing platform in [[Tipoca City/Legends|Tipoca City]] on Kamino, and then near wreckage from the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]],<ref name="Old Wounds" /> both locations that the Jedi [[Mission to Kamino (Separatist Crisis)/Legends|visited]] in the 2002 prequel [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']].<ref name="AOTC">{{Film|II}}</ref> 

[[File:MaulFlashbacks2.png|thumb|left|250px|In "Old Wounds," Maul hunts Kenobi across several locations he explored in the prequel trilogy, such as Kamino and Geonosis.]]
Maul also encounters a burning Anakin Skywalker on Mustafar<ref name="Old Wounds" /> following the [[Duel on Mustafar/Legends|duel]] between him and Kenobi depicted in ''Revenge of the Sith''.<ref name="ROTS" /> Maul then proceeded to Polis Massa,<ref name="Old Wounds" /> the site where Kenobi traveled after the duel and where Luke Skywalker was born in ''Revenge of the Sith''.<ref name="ROTS" /> Maul also recounts decapitating a [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] junk dealer for information,<ref name="Old Wounds" /> who would later be confirmed by Chee to be Watto,<ref name="Blog" /> who had previously [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] Anakin Skywalker in ''The Phantom Menace''<ref name="TPM" /> and directed him toward the Lars homestead in ''Attack of the Clones''.<ref name="AOTC" />

Kenobi's vision during the comic also references the events of the prequels,<ref name="Old Wounds" /> beginning with Jinn's death as depicted in ''The Phantom Menace''.<ref name="TPM" /> Both Mace Windu and Padmé Amidala's deaths occur in ''Revenge of the Sith'',<ref name="ROTS" /> and Kenobi also sees Anakin Skywalker's burning face<ref name="Old Wounds" /> as shown at the climax of the latter film.<ref name="ROTS" /> The comic ends with Lars forbidding Kenobi from returning to his farm,<ref name="Old Wounds" /> which links to the established distance between the Jedi and the moisture farmer in the [[1977]] [[original trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], in which Lars tells Luke that Kenobi is dead.<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref>

Lars's blaster rifle resembles<ref name="Old Wounds" /> a [[GRS-1 snare rifle/Legends|GRS-1 snare rifle]],<ref name="GRS-1">{{CCG|set=Dagobah Limited|cardname=Zuckuss' Snare Rifle}}</ref> which first appeared in the [[1980]] original trilogy film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] wielded by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Zuckuss/Legends|Zuckuss]]<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref> and was first identified in the [[Dagobah Limited]] expansion of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] in [[1997]].<ref name="GRS-1" /> However, Lars's weapon has a larger [[Stock/Legends|stock]] and is not used as a [[Snare rifle/Legends|snare rifle]] in the comic.<ref name="Old Wounds" />

===References in subsequent Legends media===
{{Quote|And, of course, there is that corniculate half-bot I revivified for the paltry fee of a double-necked, eight-string quetarra and one more quaint nom de guerre|Zeta Magnus, making an intended reference to Maul's survival in "Old Wounds" in "Skyewalkers: A Clone Wars Story"|<ref name="Skyewalkers">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Skyewalkers.jpg|thumb|right|200px|''SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story'', one of several works by Abel G. Peña that referenced Maul's survival in "Old Wounds"]] 
Although "Old Wounds" is considered non-canon within Legends,<ref name="SWBoards" /> writer [[Abel G. Peña]] included references to Maul's survival in it in several of his works. Peña's [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] exclusive article ''[[The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War]]'', published in 2006,<ref name="Grievous">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/86/indexp2.html|text=The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War|int=The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War}}</ref> states that after Kenobi used the [[Belbullab-22 starfighter/Legends|Belbullab-22 stafighter]] [[Soulless One/Legends|''Soulless One'']] to escape the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] was recovered by an old cyborg enemy of his<ref name="Grievous" />—intended to be Maul<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/52250872|board=Literature|thread=Abel G. Peña's "Skyewalkers" and "Lone Wolf"|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2015-03-23|time=6:26 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20230531204742/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/abel-g-pe%C3%B1as-skyewalkers-and-lone-wolf.50027998/page-6#post-52250872|quote=In the Interludium, there is a complex Easter egg that essentially ended up being contradicted by the TV series. Magnus refers to "that corniculate half-bot I revivified for the paltry fee of a double-necked, eight-string quetarra and one more quaint ''nom de guerre''." If you read this carefully, it is actually a reference to cyborg Darth Maul, who I'd already previously referred to in "The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War": the parts about a "corniculate half-bot" referred to his appearance in the comic "Old Wounds"}}</ref>—who reported the vessel to Imperial authorities.<ref name="Grievous" />

Peña's short story "[[Lone Wolf: A Tale of Obi-Wan and Luke]]," originally written in [[2007]],<ref name="Lone Wolf 2007">{{Blogspot|subdomain=abelgpena|url=2022/06/lone-wolf-tale-of-obi-wan-and-luke.html|name=The Philodoxer|text=Lone Wolf: A Tale of Obi-Wan and Luke Footnotes/Endnotes—Before Baby Yoda ... There Was Baby Luke!|date=2022-06-02}}</ref> mentioned a cyborg master of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Fomadu]] and [[Mei (Dark Jedi)|Mei]], intended to be Maul. The author stated that the character's rejection of a belief in a light or dark side of the Force reflected Maul's statements against them in "Old Wounds."<ref name=" Lone Wolf">{{JCFCite|url=posts/52381816|board=Literature|thread=Abel G. Peña's "Skyewalkers" and "Lone Wolf"|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2015-05-11|time=8:59 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20180314000518/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/abel-g-pe%C3%B1as-skyewalkers-and-lone-wolf.50027998/page-7|quote=Very good catch, my friend. Yes, that's exactly the intended implication and ties in, as you mention, to "Lord of War: The Story of General Grievous," where I initially planted the seed for "Lone Wolf" in the first place. I don't honestly know how this now squares with The Clone Wars TV series' co-opting of cyborg Maul from the Visionaries comic "Old Wounds," but my idea was, in fact, that after Maul had gotten resurrected somehow (and, as I said previously, I attributed this to Zeta Magnus in SkyeWalkers) he went out and got himself some apprentices and created a non-binary interpretation of the Force that is here expounded upon as the Ur-dreamers' philosophy (per cyborg Maul's statements to Obi-Wan in "Old Wounds").}}</ref> Peña's novella ''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]'', which was written from [[2008]] to [[2011]],<ref name="Reddit">{{Reddit|url=StarWars/comments/1uqc2b/hi_reddit_i_am_abel_g_pe%C3%B1a_star_wars_writer/cel1ayd|thread=Hi Reddit, I am Abel G. Peña, Star Wars writer, philodoxer, Jedi Knight. Ask me anything.|user=abelgpena|date=2014-01-08|quote=That includes the aforementioned Star Wars novella SkyeWalkers I spent about three years on--the reason I was absent from publishing between 2008-2011. |archiveurl=https://archive.md/bL9Vb}}</ref> also included a reference<ref name="JCF" /> when the [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Zeta Magnus]] mentioned reviving a [[Unidentified horned cyborg|horned cyborg]].<ref name="Skyewalkers" /> Both "Lone Wolf" and "Skyewalkers" were unpublished until [[2015]].<ref name="LostMissions">{{SW|url=news/the-other-lost-missions-rare-clone-wars-comics-and-literature-part-2|text=The Other Lost Missions: Rare Clone Wars Comics and Literature, Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref>

==Legacy==
{{Quote|I led him to you. Let me make it right.''"<br />"''That is not your responsibility. I will mend this old wound.|Ezra Bridger and Obi-Wan Kenobi on fighting Maul in ''Star Wars Rebels''|<ref name="Twin Suns">{{Rebels|Twin Suns (episode)}}</ref>}}
[[File:Maul_comparison.jpg|thumb|left|300px|McBride's design for Darth Maul in "Old Wounds" (left) inspired the character's appearance in ''The Clone Wars'' (right).]]
The idea of a resurrected Darth Maul was considered but ultimately abandoned<ref name="Ask Lobot">{{InsiderCite|129|Ask Lobot}}</ref> for the 2008 [[Star Wars video games|video game]]<ref name="TFU">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref> ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]].''<ref name="Ask Lobot" /> During production of the [[The Clone Wars: Season Three|third season]] of ''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'', George Lucas decided to revive Maul,<ref name="Insider 134">{{InsiderCite|134|Behind and Beyond the Battle Lines}}</ref> which was first alluded to in the episode "[[Witches of the Mist]],"<ref name="Witches">{{TCW|Witches of the Mist}}</ref> which aired on [[January 21]], 2011.<ref name="TCW Episode Guide">''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Episode Guide]]''</ref> In that same year, [[Sideshow Collectibles]] released a premium format action figure of Maul's appearance from "Old Wounds."<ref name="Blaster">{{InsiderCite|120|Blaster}}</ref> The figure included detachable robes and an alternate head featuring Maul's damaged horns from the comic.<ref name="Sideshow">{{SideshowCite|set=Sideshow Collectibles|text=Sideshow Exclusive|pack=Darth Maul with Mechanical Legs|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/SSC-DarthMaul2.asp}}</ref> Maul's ''The Clone Wars'' appearance with cybernetic legs, which debuted in the [[The Clone Wars: Season Four|season four]] episode "[[Revenge (episode)|Revenge]]"<ref name="Revenge trivia gallery">{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/revenge-trivia-gallery|text="Revenge" Trivia Gallery}}</ref> on [[March 14]], [[2012]],<ref name="TCW Episode Guide" /> was designed by [[Kilian Plunkett]]<ref name="Revenge episode guide">{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/revenge-episode-guide|text="Revenge" Episode Guide | The Clone Wars}}</ref> based on Maul's look from "Old Wounds." A comparison between McBride's concept image of Maul and the character's ''The Clone Wars'' design was included in the StarWars.com [[The Clone Wars Episode Guides|episode guide]] for "Revenge."<ref name="Revenge trivia gallery" />

The [[Star Wars Rebels Season Three|third season]] of the canon animated television series ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]'' featured a plotline in which [[Maul]] tracked down [[Obi-Wan Kenobi|Kenobi]] during the [[Imperial Era]]. The Sith's motivation was first revealed in the third episode of the season, "[[The Holocrons of Fate]]," in which he was overjoyed to discover that the Jedi was alive and that he could have his revenge.<ref name="Holocrons">{{Rebels|The Holocrons of Fate}}</ref> The episode aired on [[October 1]], [[2016]].<ref name="Insider 174">{{InsiderCite|174|Star Wars Rebels Season Three Episode Guide|''Star Wars Rebels'' Season Three Episode Guide}}</ref> Maul eventually arrived on [[Tatooine]] to [[Mission to Tatooine (Imperial Era)|confront]] Kenobi in the episode "[[Twin Suns (episode)|Twin Suns]],"<ref name="Twin Suns" /> which aired on [[March 18]], [[2017]].<ref name="Insider 174" /> Before their [[lightsaber duel]], Kenobi tells the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|rebel]] [[Ezra Bridger]] that it is his own responsibility to mend the "old wound" of Maul. The brief duel ends with Maul's death by Kenobi's [[Obi-Wan Kenobi's third lightsaber|lightsaber]].<ref name="Twin Suns" />
